message:
fix(process): bound execvp's caller-supplied `file` read (bd-z4k96 class)

REVIEW round 5: execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's
`file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr without a length bound. A non-NUL-
terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked
arbitrary process memory looking for a NUL — same info-leak / segfault
class as the inet_pton fix in 0c0b59f1.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell
built-ins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec
helpers) hits this path with a caller-supplied string.

Fix: add the standard read_bounded_cstr helper and use it in place
of CStr::from_ptr; on None, return -1 with errno=ENOENT (the same
sentinel the empty-pathname branch already uses).

Full abi suite: 3120/3120 pass; 58/58 process_abi_test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
